objective: Determine where current Sybil FBA materially improves maker, trader, and shared-budget outcomes relative to fair CLOB baselines, and where it does not.

implementation.accounting: integer nanos and protocol quantity units; floating point is analysis-only

freeze_assertions: The immutable implementation revision was pushed before this protocol was created.
No seed in run_seeds had been executed or inspected when this protocol was created.
Axis selection used only development seeds 0 through 31 and is recorded in the development protocol and evidence plan.
The complete metric table, failures, zero fills, caps, timeouts, and verifier-invalid rows remain in the evidence denominator.

comparators.mechanics: Actual production retained-cash solver, uniform batch clearing, no intra-batch time priority, and atomic shared MM budget. The current live MM bundle is one-shot and deferred; after acknowledgement it cannot use the resting-order cancel path before its eligible batch.
Counterfactual FBA in which an acknowledged MM bundle can be atomically canceled or replaced before cutoff. This is a future-design sensitivity and must never be labeled current Sybil behavior.
Price-time priority, immediate execution, explicit cancel/replace latency, and worst-case capital reserved for every displayed firm maker quote.

Same CLOB, but quotes may span the catalog under one account risk limit; serial fills consume the limit and later maker fills are canceled. This is a strong executable-coverage baseline, not equivalent firm simultaneous depth.

paired_input_contract: same fundamental path and shock phase
same participant information times and network latencies
same order valuations, quantities, and expiry intent
same maker cash budget and initial inventory
mechanism-specific cancellation capabilities are explicit outputs, not silently equalized
mechanism-specific processing only after messages reach the venue

episode_families.purpose: Measure stale-quote loss, spread capture, trader execution, price discovery, and delay in quiet and jump windows.
Measure firm quoted coverage, executable coverage, fill allocation, budget use, and trader welfare across many markets.

axis_selection_provenance.rule: Retain qualitative boundaries and remove only development-diagnostic interpolation levels.
retained_boundaries: 500 ms current binary cadence, 1 s intermediate cadence, and 10 s current Compose cadence
1 cent, 5 cent, and 10 cent quote half-spreads
maker reaction tied with, faster than, and slower than taker reaction
one trader, two traders that exhaust maker size, and eight-trader high-demand flow
uniform, head-heavy, and independently shuffled flow

run_seeds.selection: First 128 seeds of the untouched range declared by the development protocol, frozen before execution.
run_seeds.reuse: Never reuse these consumed seeds for confirmatory evidence after the first complete run.

uncertainty.multiple_views: Report the complete preregistered metric table; do not select only favorable metrics.

materiality.decision_rule: Call a configuration-level difference material only when the paired point estimate exceeds the declared threshold and its 95% interval excludes zero. Do not collapse maker protection, trader quality, coverage, and delay into a single score.

failure_policy.conditional_metrics: Leave a conditional metric undefined when either engine has no defined value; always report the defined and undefined pair counts beside it.

historical_case_study.selection: Analyze the complete January 1-31 event family. The previously cited January 3, 10, 11, and 12 transactions are labeled known cases, not an unbiased sample.
limits: Public trade rows do not reconstruct canceled quotes or venue arrival times.
Resting-side counterparties are not assumed to be professional market makers.
Settlement or later-price markout is gross of fees, hedges, rebates, and inventory history.
No historical FBA counterfactual price is identified.
